story.tasks:
- Task 1: Install and configure eslint-plugin-import (AC: #1) - Subtask 1.1: Install eslint-plugin-import package - Subtask 1.2: Update .eslintrc.js to include import plugin - Subtask 1.3: Configure import/order rule with 8-category pattern - Subtask 1.4: Test: Verify plugin loads correctly
- Task 2: Configure import order rules (AC: #2) - Subtask 2.1: Define import groups in .eslintrc.js - Subtask 2.2: Set 'newlines-between': 'always' for group separation - Subtask 2.3: Configure alphabetization within groups - Subtask 2.4: Test: Verify correct import order enforcement
- Task 3: Enable auto-fix and VS Code integration (AC: #3) - Subtask 3.1: Update .vscode/settings.json for import organization on save - Subtask 3.2: Configure 'source.organizeImports' code action - Subtask 3.3: Add 'eslint.autoFixOnSave' for import violations - Subtask 3.4: Test: Verify imports auto-organize on file save
- Task 4: Add import check to CI/CD pipeline (AC: #4) - Subtask 4.1: Add import order check to .github/workflows/ci.yml - Subtask 4.2: Run npm run lint with --ext .ts to catch import violations - Subtask 4.3: Test: Verify CI/CD blocks on import order violations - Subtask 4.4: Test: Verify all checks pass when imports are correct

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: - AC 1: ESLint plugin: eslint-plugin-import - AC 2: Import order rules (hrsync-backend pattern): - 1. Libraries (external packages) - 2. DTOs - 3. Services (other modules) - 4. Repositories - 5. Entities - 6. Interfaces - 7. Enums - 8. Events - AC 3: Auto-fix on save - AC 4: CI/CD: Import order check

artifacts.docs:
- path: docs/epics/epic-12-code-quality-standards.md title: Epic 12: Code Quality & Standards section: Story 12.5: Import Organization Rules snippet: Story defines eslint-plugin-import configuration and 8-category import order pattern from hrsync-backend. AC 1-4 specify installation, configuration, auto-fix, and CI/CD integration.
- path: docs/tech-spec-epic-12.md title: Epic Technical Specification: Code Quality & Standards section: AC 12.5: Import Organization Rules snippet: Detailed technical requirements for eslint-plugin-import with import/order rule configuration. Includes 8-category grouping system and integration requirements.
- path: docs/tech-spec-epic-12.md title: Epic Technical Specification: Code Quality & Standards section: Import Organization Workflow snippet: 8-category import order workflow: Libraries → DTOs → Services → Repositories → Entities → Interfaces → Enums → Events. Alphabetical sorting within groups with newline separation.
- path: docs/stories/12-4-typescript-strict-mode.md title: Story 12.4: TypeScript Strict Mode section: Learnings from Previous Story snippet: Previous story established type assertion patterns for Prisma mocks and external libraries. Provides configuration files to update and integration strategy for CI/CD quality gates.
artifacts.code:
- path: .eslintrc.js kind: configuration symbol: ESLint Configuration lines: 1-50 reason: Base ESLint configuration from Story 12-1 that will be extended with import/order rules
- path: .vscode/settings.json kind: configuration symbol: VS Code Settings lines: 1-20 reason: VS Code integration settings that will be enhanced with import organization on save
- path: .husky/pre-commit kind: git-hook symbol: Pre-commit Hook lines: 1-15 reason: Pre-commit hook from Story 12-3 that will add import order check to quality gate
- path: .github/workflows/ci.yml kind: workflow symbol: CI Pipeline lines: 1-60 reason: CI/CD pipeline from Epic-11 that will add import check stage after type-check
- path: tsconfig.json kind: configuration symbol: TypeScript Configuration lines: 1-30 reason: TypeScript strict mode configuration from Story 12-4 that import organization complements
artifacts.dependencies: - eslint: ^9.18.0 - @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin: ^8.20.0 - @typescript-eslint/parser: ^8.20.0 - eslint-plugin-import: TBD (Epic-12) - prettier: ^3.4.2 - typescript: ^5.7.3 - husky: TBD (Epic-12)

story-context.constraints: - 8-category import order pattern (Libraries → DTOs → Services → Repositories → Entities → Interfaces → Enums → Events) - Alphabetical sorting within each import category - Newline separation between import groups - Auto-fix enabled for developer productivity - Must integrate with existing Epic-12 quality standards (ESLint, Prettier, Husky, TypeScript strict mode) - CI/CD quality gate: Import violations block merges - VS Code integration: Organize imports on save - Pre-commit hook compatibility: < 10 second execution requirement
story-context.interfaces: - name: import/order rule kind: ESLint rule configuration signature: import/order: ["error", { "groups": ["builtin", "external", "internal", "parent", "sibling", "index"], "alphabetize": { "order": "asc" }, "newlines-between": "always" }] path: .eslintrc.js

tests.standards: Testing follows Epic-9 patterns: Unit tests for configuration validation, integration tests for rule enforcement, E2E tests for commit workflow. Uses Jest framework with TypeScript strict mode compatibility from Story 12-4.
tests.locations: test/**/*.spec.ts for unit tests, test/**/*.e2e-spec.ts for E2E tests. All test files use TypeScript strict mode patterns established in Story 12-4.
tests.ideas: - Test 1: Verify eslint-plugin-import installation and plugin registration (AC: #1) - Test 2: Validate 8-category import order pattern enforcement (AC: #2) - Test 3: Confirm imports auto-organize on save in VS Code (AC: #3) - Test 4: Verify CI/CD pipeline blocks on import order violations (AC: #4) - Test 5: Integration test with pre-commit hook: Violation blocks commit - Test 6: Test alphabetization within import groups - Test 7: Test newline separation between groups - Test 8: E2E test: Complete workflow from violation → auto-fix → commit allowed
